title: Race-Lap Ranking Specialist Blended With Pointwise Hazard
summary: ROC AUC rewards ordering, and pit decisions are inherently relative within a race-lap snapshot: who among the current field is most likely to stop next.
rationale: ROC AUC rewards ordering, and pit decisions are inherently relative within a race-lap snapshot: who among the current field is most likely to stop next. A pairwise/listwise ranking expert on each lap can learn these local comparisons even when absolute probabilities are hard to calibrate.
implementation_hint: Create groups at (Year, Race, LapNumber) or (Year, Race, LapNumber, compound regime) and train a LambdaRank/RankNet-style model or pairwise classifier to rank drivers by next-lap pit likelihood. Blend its OOF logits with the main pointwise model, then apply a lightweight beta calibrator only to the final blended score if submission sharpness matters.
expected_effect: Often improves AUC by correcting local ordering errors inside the same race context, especially when several drivers sit in overlapping pit windows.
risk: Group sizes are small and positive labels per lap may be sparse, so the ranking expert may be unstable unless heavily regularized and used only as a complementary ensemble member.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
